backtest.title: Portfolio Backtest

backtest.swrPwrDesc: SWR/PWR vs retirement years (10/20/30/40Y are actual data, rest are linear interpolation)
backtest.survivalDesc: Survival rate = rolling windows where ending value > 0; Preservation rate = rolling windows where ending value >= initial value
backtest.withdrawalSuccessDesc: Success rate of portfolio value remaining > 0 at end of period, across different withdrawal rates (3%-5%) and retirement years
backtest.seoDesc: This platform is a portfolio backtesting tool supporting ETFs, stocks, funds, synthetic assets and custom series. Compare multiple portfolios over the same historical period, test rebalancing rules, simulate contributions or withdrawals, and view drawdown, rolling returns, correlations and withdrawal results.
backtest.seoModelableDesc: Portfolio weights, date ranges, rebalancing schedules, cashflows, inflation, drag and withdrawal assumptions.
backtest.seoViewableDesc: CAGR, MWRR, volatility, max drawdown, Sharpe/Sortino/Calmar ratios, rolling metrics, seasonality, correlations and retirement withdrawal statistics.

optimizer.seoDesc: The backtest optimizer searches for the optimal rebalance frequency and threshold combination in the parameter space, supporting multi-objective optimization and historical constraints.

optimizer.seoDesc: Portfolio optimization tool helps you search for optimal asset allocation instead of manual trial and error. Enter a set of ticker symbols, set weight constraints, choose optimization objective, and let the system search for the portfolio that best fits historical data and constraints.
optimizer.seoObjectiveDesc: Maximize Sharpe ratio, minimize volatility, maximize return, with support for multiple constraints, short selling, holding limits and genetic algorithm solver.
optimizer.seoOutputDesc: Optimal weight allocation, expected return, expected volatility, Sharpe ratio, efficient frontier scatter plot and constraints summary.

monteCarlo.seoOutputDesc: Distribution statistics table (Summary), portfolio value range chart, success probability curves, multi-metric distribution histograms, representative scenario paths.

seo.desc: Compare the impact of different rebalancing frequencies on the long-term performance of the same portfolio. Run parallel backtests across multiple rebalancing frequencies to directly observe differences in CAGR, volatility, max drawdown, Sharpe ratio, and Sortino ratio.
seo.analyzableDesc: Impact of daily/weekly/monthly/quarterly/annual rebalancing on portfolio return and risk, with deviation band scanning support.
seo.offsetScanDesc: Scan different offset days (0-20) at a selected frequency to observe the impact of rebalancing timing on returns.

seo.desc: The Efficient Frontier tool helps you extend from a single "optimal" portfolio to a complete historical tested portfolio spectrum. It generates a series of portfolios that trade off between return and risk.
seo.visualizationDesc: Scatter plot showing risk-return tradeoffs, colored by Sharpe ratio from red to green, with max Sharpe portfolio highlighted. Click to view weight details.
seo.constraintsDesc: Supports rebalancing frequency, cash allocation, return/risk objectives, solver selection, minimum inclusion weight, and other constraint settings.

seo.desc: The Tactical Allocation tool builds trading signals based on technical indicators (SMA/EMA/RSI/MACD/Bollinger Bands/Momentum), supports multi-signal aggregation (voting/weighted average/ranking) to generate dynamic weight switching strategies, runs historical backtests against equal-weight benchmarks, and provides What-If real-time signal queries and email alert configuration.
seo.configurableDesc: Technical indicator parameters, signal trigger conditions, target weights, aggregation method, rebalancing frequency.
seo.viewableDesc: Growth curve, statistics, signal switching history, real-time price and signal status.

lumpSumDca.title: Lump Sum vs DCA
lumpSumDca.errEmptyAssets: Please add at least one ticker

conclusion.title: Conclusion Analysis
conclusion.lumpSumWinsAfter: has a higher final value ({{lsValue}} vs {{dcaValue}}), exceeding by {{pct}}%. However, Lump Sum's max drawdown ({{lsMdd}}) is typically larger than DCA's ({{dcaMdd}}), bearing greater psychological pressure in falling markets.
conclusion.dcaWinsAfter: has a higher final value ({{dcaValue}} vs {{lsValue}}), exceeding by {{pct}}%. DCA reduces average cost through batch purchases, achieving better returns in falling markets.
conclusion.riskWarningLumpSum: Although Lump Sum performs better in this historical period, this is a hindsight result. Lump Sum carries greater timing risk at entry; entering at market peaks may cause significant losses. While DCA has a lower final value, it reduces timing risk through staggered entries, suitable for investors with lower risk tolerance.
conclusion.riskWarningDca: DCA performs better in this historical period, indicating the market experienced significant volatility or declines during this time. DCA reduces average cost through batch purchases, but if the market continues to rise, Lump Sum typically achieves higher returns. Investment decisions should consider personal risk tolerance and market judgment.
conclusion.historicalDisclaimer: Historical performance does not guarantee future returns.

seo.desc: Compare lump sum investment with dollar-cost averaging (DCA) on the same portfolio. Lump sum invests fully at the start, while DCA invests in batches, observing the final value and risk characteristics of both strategies under different market conditions.
seo.configurableDesc: Growth curve, final value, CAGR, volatility, max drawdown, Sharpe ratio of lump sum vs monthly/quarterly DCA.
seo.strategyDesc: DCA divides the initial capital into several periods with equal amounts each period; uninvested funds can optionally be placed in short-term Treasury bills (T-Bill) for risk-free returns.

results.title: Fama-French Three-Factor Regression Results
results.alphaDesc: Portfolio excess return (annualized); positive means outperforming the factor model's expectation
results.betaDesc: Market sensitivity; 1.0 means moving in sync with the market
results.smbDesc: Size factor loading; positive tilts toward small-cap stocks
results.hmlDesc: Value factor loading; positive tilts toward value stocks
results.rSquaredDesc: Model explanatory power; closer to 1 means factors explain returns more fully
results.mockNotice: Factor data sourced from Kenneth French database (simulated data). The full version will integrate real-time Fama-French factor data.

seo.desc: Use the Fama-French three-factor model (MKT-RF, SMB, HML) to run regression analysis on a portfolio, decompose return sources, and compute Alpha, Beta, size and value factor loadings, as well as R-squared and residuals.
seo.analyzableDesc: Contribution of market, size, and value factors to portfolio excess returns, plus the manager's Alpha ability.
seo.factorDesc: MKT-RF: market excess return; SMB: small-cap minus big-cap; HML: value minus growth. Data sourced from Kenneth French database.

seo.desc: The Goal Optimizer runs Monte Carlo simulations based on historical return distributions to compute the probability of reaching your financial goal, and recommends an asset allocation and regular contribution plan.
seo.analyzableDesc: Set target amount, initial capital, and time horizon; simulate thousands of future paths based on the historical return characteristics of your asset allocation.
seo.outputDesc: Probability of reaching the goal, final-value probability distribution curve, median/P10/P90 optimal path, expected return, and required regular contribution.

asset.sectionInfo: Add 2 or more ticker symbols; PCA analyzes their daily returns

params.numComponentsHint: Leave empty to keep all components (equals the number of assets)

seo.desc: Principal Component Analysis (PCA) tool performs dimensionality reduction on daily returns of multiple assets, extracting the main driving factors to help you understand the risk structure and common sources of variation in your portfolio.
seo.analyzableDesc: Eigenvalues, cumulative variance explained, loading matrix (each asset's contribution to principal components), and principal component scores.
seo.scenarioDesc: Identify the main risk factors of a portfolio, dimensionality-reduction visualization, factor model construction, and diversification analysis.

letf.title: LETF Slippage Analysis
letf.errEmptyTickers: Please enter both the leveraged ETF and benchmark index symbols

etf.sectionInfo: Leveraged ETF (e.g. TQQQ/UPRO) and the corresponding benchmark index (e.g. QQQ/SPY); the leverage multiplier must match the ETF's actual leverage

seo.desc: Leveraged ETF (LETF) slippage analysis tool quantifies the deviation between the expected and actual returns of a leveraged ETF relative to its benchmark, revealing the decay drag of holding leveraged ETFs long-term.
seo.analyzableDesc: Daily/cumulative slippage curve, annual decay, effective vs nominal leverage, and benchmark vs LETF return comparison.
seo.scenarioDesc: Evaluate the decay cost of holding leveraged ETFs long-term, verify leverage compounding bias, and compare the tracking efficiency of different LETFs.

about.title: About the Platform
about.intro: The Backtest Platform is an open-source portfolio backtesting tool supporting multiple markets (US/China/HK/Japan/Europe etc.), multiple currencies (USD/CNY), inflation adjustment, FX conversion, Monte Carlo simulation, portfolio optimization, and efficient frontier analysis. All data is stored locally — no registration required, no privacy leakage risk.

features.localDeployDesc: All data stored locally, never uploaded to the cloud — privacy-first
features.multiMarketDesc: US, China, HK, Japan, Europe and other major global markets
features.highPerfDesc: Rust backtest engine + Go data service, millisecond response
features.richDataDesc: 12,000+ tickers, including price, fundamentals, and factor data

about.techStackContent: Rust (backtest engine) · Go (data service) · TypeScript (frontend + API glue) · React + Recharts

limits.intro: This is a self-hosted edition with no cloud restrictions. The following limits apply only to data fetching and compute resources:
limits.portfolioCountDesc: Backtest any number of portfolios concurrently
limits.backtestRangeDesc: From earliest available data to date
limits.concurrentDesc: Go engine uses goroutines for parallelism
limits.noticeContent: Data fetching is subject to third-party API rate limits. yfinance defaults to 30 req/min; iTick API requires registration for a token. Prefer incremental updates over full refreshes to avoid triggering rate limits.

upgrade.intro: The current version already includes all core features. The following are optional enhancement plans:
perfPlanFeatures: More CPU cores → faster Monte Carlo

changelog.title: Changelog
changelog.intro: Records major changes across platform versions, sorted in reverse chronological order. Changes are categorized as
changelog.gitHistoryHint: For the full commit history, see the project's Git repository.

changes.text: Added 10+ testfol.io-aligned tool pages (tactical allocation, PCA, signal analysis, etc.)
Added four auxiliary pages: help center, changelog, pricing, account
Refactored top navigation into grouped dropdown menus; added engine status indicator
Portfolio editor now supports asymmetric bands, glidepath, and cashflow modeling
Statistics tables support column-header sorting; charts support zoom and CSV export
Fixed infinite polling on data engine page causing "loading data" hang
Fixed missing prompt when Rust engine silently degraded
Added security hardening (Helmet, CORS, rate limiting)
Added structured logging (pino) and request tracing
Added Docker containerized deployment and CI pipeline
Comprehensively fixed TypeScript type errors; enabled strict mode
API layer added input validation and error-handling middleware
Fixed multiple memory leaks and unhandled Promise rejections
Added HK, Japan, and Europe market data support
Added BaoStock TCP direct connection for China A-shares
Added iTick API real-time quotes integration
Refactored data cache layer to support incremental updates and avoid rate limits
FX conversion now supports USD/CNY dual-currency switching
Integrated Rust Actix-Web backtest engine (HTTP service mode)
Implemented three-tier engine fallback strategy (Rust → Node.js → static computation)
Added Monte Carlo simulation and efficient frontier analysis
Backtest performance improved 10x; supports rayon parallel computation
Fixed rebalance threshold calculation precision issue
Portfolio backtest main page with 16 analysis tabs
Asset analysis and factor regression features
Portfolio optimizer and efficient frontier visualization
Inflation adjustment and FX conversion
UI aligned with testfol.io design language; introduced CSS variable theme system

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.intro: We welcome your feedback, suggestions, and bug reports. Please reach out via the following channels.
contact.githubNotConfigured: GitHub repository link not yet configured

methodology.title: Backtest Methodology
methodology.desc: This platform uses time-weighted returns (TWR) for backtesting, supporting both periodic and threshold rebalancing modes. All returns are compounded as log returns and converted to annualized metrics.

rebalModes.periodic: Periodic Rebalancing: Restore portfolio weights to target proportions at fixed frequencies (monthly/quarterly/annually).
rebalModes.threshold: Threshold Rebalancing: Trigger rebalancing when asset weights deviate from target by more than a set threshold (e.g., 5%).
rebalModes.buyHold: Buy and Hold: No rebalancing after initial setup; let weights drift freely with the market.

cagr.desc: The annualized geometric mean return over the entire backtest period — the most core long-term return metric.

sharpe.desc: Excess return per unit of total risk. R_f is the risk-free rate (default 0); σ_p is the annualized standard deviation of returns.

sortino.desc: Similar to Sharpe, but uses only downside volatility as the risk measure — does not penalize upside volatility, better aligning with investor intuition.

mdd.desc: The largest peak-to-trough decline in history — measures the worst-case loss magnitude.

volatility.desc: Daily return standard deviation annualized over 252 trading days — measures portfolio return volatility.

calmar.desc: Annualized return per unit of maximum drawdown — reflects the reward for taking drawdown risk.

beta.desc: Systematic risk exposure relative to a benchmark index. β=1 means moving in sync with the benchmark; β>1 is more aggressive.

alpha.desc: Excess return after accounting for systematic risk — measures active management ability.

data.title: Data Sources
data.desc: The platform supports multiple data sources; all data is cached locally in a SQLite database. After the first query, the cache is preferred to reduce API calls.

sources.name: Yahoo Finance (yfinance)
sources.note: Default data source, 30 req/min rate limit, suitable for historical daily data

sources.name: iTick API
sources.note: Requires registration for a token; provides more real-time price data

sources.name: BaoStock
sources.note: TCP direct connection; covers historical K-lines and fundamentals for SH and SZ markets

sources.name: TradingView Screener
sources.note: Used for fundamental screening and factor regression analysis

data.updateStrategyContent: Prefer incremental updates over full refreshes to avoid triggering third-party API rate limits. The data engine automatically records the last update time and only fetches missing trading days. FX and inflation data are updated monthly.

items.q: Do backtest results differ from live trading?
items.a: Backtests use historical data and do not account for slippage, market impact, or trading latency; past performance does not guarantee future returns. We recommend using Monte Carlo simulation to assess strategy robustness.

items.q: Why doesn't my portfolio weight sum to 100%?
items.a: The weight input accepts any numeric value; the platform auto-normalizes at backtest time. However, a red warning appears when weights deviate significantly from 100% — adjust close to 100% for accurate results.

items.q: How many Monte Carlo iterations should I set?
items.a: Generally 1,000 iterations suffice for a stable statistical distribution. More iterations improve precision but extend compute time; self-hosted deployments have no cap and can be tuned based on CPU performance.
After enabling "Inflation Adjustment" in the parameter panel, the platform uses CPI data to convert nominal returns into real returns. CPI data for major global markets is supported.

items.q: What happens when the Go engine is unavailable?
items.a: To ensure result correctness, compute endpoints (backtest/Monte Carlo/optimization) follow a fail-closed strategy: when the Go engine is unavailable, they return 503 with a retry-after hint, rather than approximate results inconsistent with the main engine. The navbar status indicator shows the current engine state.
All data is stored in a local SQLite database; nothing is uploaded to the cloud. Portfolio configurations and preferences are saved in the browser's localStorage.

cagr.formula: Formula: Final Value = Initial Value × (1 + Monthly Return)^Months + Monthly Contribution × [((1 + Monthly Return)^Months - 1) / Monthly Return]
cagr.title: CAGR Calculator

leverage.decayFormula: Decay Formula: Total Decay = (1 - (1 - Annual Drag)^Years) × 100%
leverage.optionFormula: Option Formula: Leverage Ratio = (Delta × Underlying Price + Option Price) / (Option Price × Contract Multiplier)

page.featureAdvancedDesc: Two-fund, risk contribution, SWR and other advanced calculators

swr.formula: Formula: SWR ≈ (Expected Return - Risk Premium × Volatility²) / (1 + Risk Premium × Volatility²)
